title: Scorzonera czerepanovii
intro: Scorzonera czerepanovii, also known as Czerpanov's Scorzonera, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing pain and improving digestive function. The bioactive constituents of Scorzonera czerepanovii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Scorzonera czerepanovii is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, ulcers, and gastritis.
taxonomy: Scorzonera czerepanovii belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is also known as the Czerpanov's scorzonera. Its classification as a species is within the genus Scorzonera. Scorzonera czerepanovii is a plant species native to Central Asia. It is a member of the subfamily Carduoideae.
